purpose: Extract non-tag fields only. This KM must not classify SpecificTopic, ApplicableSectors, or ClosestESRSTopics.

fields.definition: The most recent source-supported amendment, consolidation, update, or last-change date reflected by the instrument record.
fields.extract_when: A last modified, last amended, consolidated-through, updated-on, or latest-change date is explicit in metadata or text.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Only adoption, signature, publication, or entry-into-force dates are present; return null rather than guessing.
fields.normalization: Return YYYY-MM-DD when the exact date is available; preserve source-supported partial dates only if the downstream schema allows them.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Date_LastChange under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Date_LastChange or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Primary jurisdiction or source scope of the legislation record.
fields.extract_when: The document or metadata identifies the issuing jurisdiction, legal source scope, or official legal system.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not use ESG topic, policy area, sector, or subject matter as Topic1.
fields.normalization: Return English jurisdiction names such as European Union, Netherlands, Australia, United Kingdom, or a comparable jurisdiction label.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Topic1 under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Topic1 or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Secondary source-supported descriptor that narrows the legal/policy domain under the jurisdiction.
fields.extract_when: The title, metadata, or scope clearly identifies a domain such as Environment, Employment, Consumer Protection, Data Protection, Financial Markets, Energy, or Agriculture.
fields.do_not_extract_when: The domain is ambiguous or would require ESG inference from tag outputs; return null.
fields.normalization: Return concise English descriptors; translate non-English descriptors into English.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Topic2 under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Topic2 or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Tertiary source-supported descriptor that further refines Topic2.
fields.extract_when: The source clearly supports a narrower domain, instrument family, product class, affected activity, or implementation area.
fields.do_not_extract_when: A third-level topic would be speculative, redundant, or inferred only from benchmark/tag rules; return null.
fields.normalization: Return concise English descriptors grounded in the source.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Topic3 under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Topic3 or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Concise English display title derived from the official title and suitable for a spreadsheet or UI.
fields.extract_when: An official title or common short title is present.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not invent a title that adds legal effects or topics not in the source.
fields.normalization: Translate generic legal words and subject matter into English; preserve law numbers, CELEX numbers, act names, article numbers, and proper nouns.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports ShortTitle under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for ShortTitle or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Official full title of the instrument exactly as stated in the source language or official metadata.
fields.extract_when: The source provides a formal title, full title, official name, or citation title.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not translate, summarize, or normalize unless the source itself gives an official English title.
fields.normalization: Preserve punctuation, legal identifiers, dates, and language as stated.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports OriginalTitle under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for OriginalTitle or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Brief English plain-language summary of what the instrument does and what subject matter it regulates.
fields.extract_when: The title, scope, purpose, recitals, summary, or operative provisions support a concise description.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not use generic ESG boilerplate or infer legal effects not supported by the source.
fields.normalization: Write one or two concise sentences in English; translate non-English source content by meaning, not word-for-word.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports ShortDescription under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for ShortDescription or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Canonical legal instrument type assigned from the downstream controlled taxonomy.
fields.extract_when: The official title or metadata identifies the legal form, such as Regulation, Directive, Decision, Act, Decree, Order, Rule, Standard, Code, Convention, Treaty, or Guidance.
fields.do_not_extract_when: The legal form is not explicit and cannot be confidently inferred from citation style.
fields.normalization: Map non-English legal-form words to the canonical English enum used by the schema; e.g., Wet/Law -> Act, Verordening -> Regulation, Richtlijn -> Directive, Besluit/Koninklijk Besluit -> Decision or Decree depending on legal form.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports TypeofRegulation under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for TypeofRegulation or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Source URL or equivalent official source reference for the legislation record.
fields.extract_when: source_url, final_url, official URL, citation link, or source reference is present in the input metadata.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not infer or construct URLs from titles, domains, or identifiers if absent.
fields.normalization: Copy the source reference exactly from metadata.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Source under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Source or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Array of instruments, acts, regulations, protocols, or measures explicitly stated to amend, supplement, develop, implement, or modify the instrument.
fields.extract_when: The source explicitly lists amendments, corrigenda, implementing/delegated acts, protocols, development measures, or instruments that modify/develop the record.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not infer related instruments from citations, references, or subject similarity unless the relationship is explicit.
fields.normalization: Return an array of official identifiers/titles; translate only generic descriptors for readability while preserving citations and names.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Amendedordevelopedbyifany under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Amendedordevelopedbyifany or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Date on which the instrument was originally adopted, signed, enacted, made, or issued.
fields.extract_when: The official title, metadata, signature block, adoption clause, or citation states the original adoption/signature date.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not use publication, entry-into-force, amendment, consolidation, last-change, or application dates.
fields.normalization: Return YYYY-MM-DD when exact date is available. For EU instruments, the date in the formal title 'of [date]' is often the adoption date.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Dateofadoption under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Dateofadoption or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Date on which the instrument first entered into force, became effective, or began applying.
fields.extract_when: The text states 'enters into force', 'comes into operation', 'shall apply from', 'effective date', or a comparable commencement clause.
fields.do_not_extract_when: Do not use adoption or publication date unless the source expressly states entry into force is tied to that date.
fields.normalization: Return YYYY-MM-DD when exact date is available; calculate from source text only when the rule is explicit and the publication date is known.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports EntryIntoForceDate under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for EntryIntoForceDate or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.

fields.definition: Whether the instrument itself explicitly includes concrete sanctions, penalties, offences, fines, imprisonment, administrative penalties, liability consequences, suspension, withdrawal, seizure, or comparable punitive consequences.
fields.extract_when: The document has a penalties/sanctions/offences section or states fines, criminal penalties, imprisonment, administrative penalties, punitive consequences, or comparable enforcement consequences for breach.
fields.do_not_extract_when: The source only mentions monitoring, supervision, inspections, remedies, compliance orders, permits, or enforcement without punitive consequences.
fields.normalization: Emit exactly Yes or No from the controlled enum. Use No when uncertain.

test_case.question: If the source explicitly supports Sanctions under its extract_when rule, should the extractor return the value instead of null/empty?
test_case.reasoning_check: Extract only source-supported values and normalize exactly as instructed.

test_case.question: If the source lacks evidence for Sanctions or only supports an excluded proxy, should the extractor invent a value?
test_case.reasoning_check: Return null, [], or No as directed rather than guessing.
